data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The problem: **globs expand at evaluation time**, before any targets run, so the generated file isn't included in `Compile`.

The fix is to explicitly add the generated file to the `Compile` item group after creating it, and register it in `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after generating it. This is the classic "including generated files" pattern.

Let me fix it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** MSBuild globs (`Compile` items) expand at **evaluation time**, before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the `.cs` file at **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target (after generating it), and registered it in `FileWrites` so `dotnet clean` removes it properly.
